QF.id: VI Debt Instruments and Government Securities_19

question.text: What is the maturity period of Treasury Bills?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Treasury Bills have a maturity period of up to one year, while Dated Securities have a maturity period of more than one year. **The Trap:** T-Bills come in 91-day, 182-day, and 364-day variants, so options (b) and (c) are only partial answers. **Cross-Exam Context:** Treasury Bill maturity is tested in NISM Series VI and money market certifications. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Remember: T-Bills = up to 1 year, Dated Securities = more than 1 year. This is a key distinction in government securities.
